title: Dark Deep Fried Russian Beef Dumplings
description: A yeasty, molten, date-night Russian dish featuring dark soft brown sugar, agemono-deep-fry, and is FODMAP-friendly, cooked in a Dutch oven. This recipe is perfect for a special occasion.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground beef, dark brown sugar, yeast, flour, egg, salt, and black pepper, and mix until just combined.
Knead the mixture for 5 minutes until it forms a sticky dough.
Cover the dough with plastic wrap and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Once the dough has risen, divide it into 8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Heat the vegetable oil in a Dutch oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Carefully place the dough balls into the hot oil and fry for 5 minutes on each side.
Remove the dumplings from the oil with a slotted spoon and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the dumplings hot and enjoy.
